libraries.name: Guava InternalFutureFailureAccess and InternalFutures
libraries.description:
Contains
    com.google.common.util.concurrent.internal.InternalFutureFailureAccess and
    InternalFutures. Most users will never need to use this artifact. Its
    classes is conceptually a part of Guava, but they're in this separate
    artifact so that Android libraries can use them without pulling in all of
    Guava (just as they can use ListenableFuture by depending on the
    listenablefuture artifact).

libraries.name: Guava: Google Core Libraries for Java
libraries.description:
Guava is a suite of core and expanded libraries that include
    utility classes, Google's collections, I/O classes, and
    much more.

libraries.name: Guava ListenableFuture only
libraries.description:
An empty artifact that Guava depends on to signal that it is providing
    ListenableFuture -- but is also available in a second "version" that
    contains com.google.common.util.concurrent.ListenableFuture class, without
    any other Guava classes. The idea is:

    - If users want only ListenableFuture, they depend on listenablefuture-1.0.

    - If users want all of Guava, they depend on guava, which, as of Guava
    27.0, depends on
    listenablefuture-9999.0-empty-to-avoid-conflict-with-guava. The 9999.0-...
    version number is enough for some build systems (notably, Gradle) to select
    that empty artifact over the "real" listenablefuture-1.0 -- avoiding a
    conflict with the copy of ListenableFuture in guava itself. If users are
    using an older version of Guava or a build system other than Gradle, they
    may see class conflicts. If so, they can solve them by manually excluding
    the listenablefuture artifact or manually forcing their build systems to
    use 9999.0-....

libraries.description: Compose UI primitives. This library contains the primitives that form the Compose UI Toolkit, such as drawing, measurement and layout.

libraries.description: Compose classes related to dimensions without units

libraries.name: Compose UI Text
libraries.description: Compose Text primitives and utilities

libraries.name: Compose UI Preview Tooling
libraries.description: Compose tooling library API. This library provides the API required to declare @Preview composables in user apps.

libraries.name: Compose Unit
libraries.description: Compose classes for simple units

libraries.name: Compose Util
libraries.description: Internal Compose utilities used by other modules

libraries.name: Kotlin Libraries bill-of-materials
libraries.description: Kotlin is a statically typed programming language that compiles to JVM byte codes and JavaScript

libraries.name: Parcelize Runtime
libraries.description: Runtime library for the Parcelize compiler plugin

libraries.name: Kotlin Reflect
libraries.description: Kotlin Full Reflection Library

libraries.name: Kotlin Stdlib
libraries.description: Kotlin Standard Library

libraries.name: Kotlin Stdlib Common
libraries.description: Kotlin Common Standard Library (legacy, use kotlin-stdlib instead)

libraries.name: Kotlin Stdlib Jdk7
libraries.description: Kotlin Standard Library JDK 7 extension

libraries.name: Kotlin Stdlib Jdk8
libraries.description: Kotlin Standard Library JDK 8 extension

libraries.name: kotlinx-collections-immutable
libraries.description: Kotlin Immutable Collections multiplatform library

libraries.description: Coroutines support libraries for Kotlin

libraries.description: Coroutines support libraries for Kotlin

libraries.description: Coroutines support libraries for Kotlin

libraries.description: Coroutines support libraries for Kotlin

libraries.description: Kotlin multiplatform serialization runtime library

libraries.description: Kotlin multiplatform serialization runtime library

libraries.description: A set of annotations used for code inspection support and code documentation.

libraries.name: markdown

libraries.name: jsoup Java HTML Parser
libraries.description: jsoup is a Java library that simplifies working with real-world HTML and XML. It offers an easy-to-use API for URL fetching, data parsing, extraction, and manipulation using DOM API methods, CSS, and xpath selectors. jsoup implements the WHATWG HTML5 specification, and parses HTML to the same DOM as modern browsers.

libraries.name: JSpecify annotations
libraries.description: An artifact of well-named and well-specified annotations to power static analysis checks
